
### exercise: holes in the model? 

::::: {.columns}

:::: {.column}

```c
void example(int a) {
    int *p;
    int *q;
    q = malloc(...);
    p = malloc(...);
    // (A)
    if (a > 0) {
        // (A1)
        p = q;
    }
    // (B)
    free(p);
    // (C)
    ...
}
```

::::

:::: {.column style="font-size:50%"}

exercise: what should state of pointer q be at C?

<table class="answer-twocol">
<tr><td>A. allocated</td><td>B. freed</td></tr>
<tr><td colspan="2">C. allocated if+only if reached via path with A1</td></tr>
<tr><td colspan="2">D. freed if+only if reached via path with A1</td></tr>
<tr><td colspan="2">E. something else?</td></tr>
</table>

::::

:::::

### clang-analyzer output 


![](/static/clang-analyze-example4){fig-alt="clang-analyzer screenshot finding an error when '*q = 1;' is placed at (C). The sequence of events identified is: memory is allocated at 'q = malloc(...)'; assuming a > 0, the true branch of the if is taken; memory is released at 'free(p)'; then memory is used after freed at '*q = 1'"}
